title: Organic Feed for Indoor Lemon Trees: What Enthusiasts Actually Use
intro: Indoor citrus needs consistent, balanced feeding year-round — organic fertilizers that enthusiasts trust tend to be slow-release or liquid concentrates with a proven track record in citrus forums, not mass-market garden center impulse buys.

items.title: Madison Citrus Nursery Specialized Citrus Tree Fertilizer 8-2-8
items.note: Semi slow-release 8-2-8 sourced and used by a working citrus nursery — the kind of pick that comes from growers who've tested it on their own trees, not from a garden center endcap.

items.title: AgroThrive Organic Liquid Fertilizer (Citrus Formulation)
items.note: Fast-acting OMRI-listed organic concentrate that shows up consistently in citrus enthusiast forums as the liquid option people reach for when they want something genuinely organic with real nutrient density.

items.title: Logee's Chelated Liquid Iron
items.note: Sold by one of the oldest greenhouse operations in the US, specifically dosed for potted citrus — the chelated formula means iron actually absorbs rather than locking up in alkaline container mix.

items.title: Madison Citrus Nursery EDTA Chelated Micronutrients
items.note: Full chelated micronutrient panel (Fe, Mn, Zn, Cu, Mo) in low-dust micro-granules that dissolve completely — the kind of granular detail in the spec sheet that serious growers look for.

warnings: Via Citrus Slow Release Fertilizer dropped — duplicate of the same product appearing on both viacitrus.com and Etsy; no meaningful differentiator and brand has no community track record to verify.
Opulent Blends Liquid Citrus Essentials and Gardenera 3-1-2 Concentrate dropped in debug mode — plausible picks but no independent community signal found to validate under the enthusiast lens.
My Perfect Plants Liquid Lemon Tree Fertilizer dropped — price field extracted as '2995' (likely $29.95 but unconfirmed), and brand has no clear enthusiast provenance.
AgroThrive URL resolves to a category/marketing page, not a specific product page — needs a direct product URL before publishing.
Madison Citrus Nursery 8-2-8 is explicitly noted as NOT organic in its product description; flagged against the collection's organic framing and should be reconsidered or noted as synthetic.
